content.problemStatement:
Define f(A)f(A)f(A) for an array AAA of length NNN as the number of elements XXX such that there exists an array CCC, also of length NNN, satisfying :


0≤Ci≤10 \le C_i \le 10≤Ci​≤1
⨁i=1N(Ai⋅Ci)=X\bigoplus_{i = 1}^{N} (A_i \cdot C_i) = X⨁i=1N​(Ai​⋅Ci​)=X, where ⊕\oplus⊕ represents the bitwise XOR operator.
This is equivalent to choosing some (possibly-empty) subsequence of AAA (the elements with Ci=1C_i = 1Ci​=1), and then their XOR should be XXX.

For example, for the array A=[1,2,3]A = [1, 2, 3]A=[1,2,3], f(A)=4f(A) = 4f(A)=4 because we can choose a valid array CCC for X=0,1,2,3X = 0, 1, 2, 3X=0,1,2,3.

You are given an integer array AAA of length NNN, and a parameter KKK.

Construct an array BBB in the following way:


Start with BBB as an empty array.
For all KKK-length sequences X1,X2,…,XKX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_KX1​,X2​,…,XK​, satisfying 1≤X1<X2<X3…<XK≤N1 \le X_1 < X_2 < X_3 \ldots < X_K \le N1≤X1​<X2​<X3​…<XK​≤N, append ⨁i=1K(AXi)\bigoplus_{i = 1}^{K} (A_{X_i})⨁i=1K​(AXi​​) to BBB.
Less formally, we take all KKK length subsequences of AAA, and append their XOR to BBB.

Find f(B)f(B)f(B).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line f(B)f(B)f(B), where BBB is the array obtained by appending the XOR of each KKK-length subsequence of AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : B=B = B= the array AAA itself as K=1K = 1K=1. And we can make X=0,1,2,3X = 0, 1, 2, 3X=0,1,2,3, so f(B)=4f(B) = 4f(B)=4.
Test Case 2 : B=[1⊕2,2⊕3,1⊕3]=[3,1,2]B = [1 \oplus 2, 2 \oplus 3, 1 \oplus 3] = [3, 1, 2]B=[1⊕2,2⊕3,1⊕3]=[3,1,2], and this still has f(B)=4f(B) = 4f(B)=4.
Test Case 3 : B=[1⊕2⊕3]=[0]B = [1 \oplus 2 \oplus 3] = [0]B=[1⊕2⊕3]=[0]. We can only make X=0X = 0X=0, so f(B)=1f(B) = 1f(B)=1.
